title: Today, Senator Dave Cortese (D-San Jose) introduced SB 692, the “Special Education Inclusion Act”, sponsored by the Santa Clara County Office of Education (SCCOE).

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Numerous studies indicate that inclusion significantly increases graduation rates and academic performance for students with disabilities.",
    "California ranks poorly in rates of inclusion for students with special needs compared to other states.",
    "Research indicates that all students do better when students with disabilities receive educational supports and services in inclusive environments and classrooms.",
    "The bill SB 692 aims to create a disincentive for schools to segregate students with special needs into separate and unequal classrooms.",
    "The bill would hold schools accountable to creating more inclusive environments for students with special needs by adding a federal 'inclusion' target to the state accountability dashboard.",
    "The bill would incentivize districts to provide general education teachers with training and professional development on teaching strategies for common disabilities such as ADHD, dyslexia, dyscalculia, and autism."
  ]
}
```
